concepts.title: Format of a Formal Letter
concepts.description: A formal letter should have the sender's address, date, inside address, subject, salutation, body, closing, signature, and postal address.

concepts.title: Use of Salutation and Closing
concepts.description: In a formal letter, use formal salutations like 'Dear Sir/Madam' or 'Respected Sir/Madam' and formal closings like 'Yours faithfully' or 'Yours sincerely'.

concepts.title: Date Format
concepts.description: In a formal letter, date should be written in the format 'Date: Day, Month, Year' (e.g. 12th March, 2023).

concepts.title: Sender's Address Format
concepts.description: To remember the format of sender's address, use the mnemonic 'Sender's Address: Name, House Number, Street, City, Pincode, State'.

concepts.title: Use of Contractions
concepts.description: In a formal letter, avoid using contractions like 'don't' or 'won't' and instead use full words like 'do not' or 'will not'.

concepts.title: Parts of a Formal Letter
concepts.description: The parts of a formal letter are: Sender's Address, Date, Inside Address, Subject, Salutation, Body, Closing, Signature, and Postal Address.

concepts.title: Use of Formal Language
concepts.description: In a formal letter, use formal language, avoid using colloquial expressions, slang, or jargon, and maintain a professional tone.

concepts.title: Use of Signatures
concepts.description: In a formal letter, use a formal signature like 'Yours faithfully' or 'Yours sincerely' followed by your name and designation.
